entry.title: End-to-End Warehouse and Quality Synchronization in SAP S/4HANA Using SAP EWM and QM
entry.content:
Abstract & Introduction:

The integration between SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) and SAP Quality Management (QM) plays a critical role in enabling efficient quality inspection, stock control, and warehouse execution processes within modern supply chain operations. In SAP S/4HANA environments, this integration ensures that quality inspection activities are tightly synchronized with inbound, outbound, and internal warehouse movements, thereby improving inventory accuracy, traceability, compliance, and operational efficiency.

This paper presents an end-to-end overview of SAP EWM and QM integration, covering both configuration activities and business process execution in SAP S/4HANA. The document explains how inspection lots are created during goods receipt processes, how quality inspection stock is managed within EWM-managed warehouses, and how inspection decisions influence subsequent warehouse activities such as put away, stock transfer, and outbound processing. It also discusses inspection-relevant master data, inspection types, stock postings, warehouse process integration, and inspection execution scenarios using embedded SAP EWM.

Objection of this document to show end to end integration between SAP EWM and QM, especially for Goods receipt against purchase order process. Normally consultant does not have very clarity on EWM and QM integration and people normally miss some configuration or master data and due to that some time inspection lot not even created.

Below Function module can be used if Inspection lot is wrongly created either wrong inspection type origin or associated inspection type

QPLEXT_INSPECTION_LOT_CREATE

Top of Form

Bottom of Form

Customization:

#1 Generate Inspection object type(IOT) versions:

Inspection object type controls the following:

  • inspection document creation,
  • inspection rules,
  • follow-up actions,
  • stock posting after UD,
  • warehouse inspection flow

This configuration step to create the active technical version of the inspection object type configuration. Activate and generate the runtime settings for this inspection object type so EWM QM processes can actually use it.

This is one of critical configuration, which I also missed and thought the previous version will work, but you should always do this step.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Basics and Integration->Generate Inspection object type versions

#2 Maintain Inspection object type versions:

This step is used to manage the configuration version, which was created in previous version.

This is one of critical configuration, which I also missed and need to select the latest version and should not miss.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Basics and Integration->Maintain Inspection object type versions

#3 Warehouse dependent IOT settings:

This setting control how a particular Inspection Object Type (IOT) behaves in a specific warehouse number.

This is also very important step, so that IOT is activated for your warehouse.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Basics and Integration->Warehouse dependent IOT settings

#4 Maintain Inspection type:

An Inspection Type in SAP Quality Management defines when and how a quality inspection should be performed for a material. It controls inspection lot creation, stock posting, and usage decision processing during business transactions like goods receipt or production. I have created inspection type ZP01 by copying inspection type 1701.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot creation->Maintain Inspection type

#5 Inspection type for origin

In SAP Quality Management, Inspection Origin identifies the business process that triggered the inspection lot creation. The system uses the inspection origin to determine which inspection type and quality process should be executed.

For EWM integration, inspection origin should always be 17.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot creation->Inspection type for origin

#6 Default values for the inspection type

Default values of an Inspection Type in SAP Quality Management are predefined settings automatically applied during inspection processing.
These settings control behavior such as automatic inspection lot creation, posting to quality stock, and usage decision requirements.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot creation-> Default values for the inspection type

#7 Define Follow-up actions for inspection results

Define Follow-Up Actions for Inspection Results in SAP Extended Warehouse Management controls what action the system should perform after Usage Decision (UD). It determines activities such as stock movement, warehouse task creation, and posting stock to unrestricted or blocked stock after inspection completion.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Inspection results ->Maintain follow-up actions

#8 Code group for Usage Decision

A Code Group for Usage Decision in SAP Quality Management is a collection of decision codes used during Usage Decision processing. It groups multiple UD codes such as Accept, Reject, Rework, or Scrap under one logical category for inspection processing.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot completion-> Edit code groups and codes for usage decision

#9 Selected set codes for Usage Decision:

Selected Set Codes for Usage Decision in SAP Quality Management are predefined Usage Decision codes grouped together for inspection processing.
They allow users to select standardized decisions such as Accept, Reject, Rework, or Scrap during UD execution.

As per my personal experience, I missed below configuration and I was not able to see any UD code, that has eaten significant time.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot completion-> Selected set codes for Usage decision

#10 Maintain Settings at plant level

Maintain Settings at Plant Level in SAP Quality Management is used to define quality management control settings specific to a plant. It controls behavior such as inspection lot creation, usage decision processing, stock posting, and quality integration for materials within that plant.

Quality Management->Basic Settings->Maintain Settings at plant level

Master data:

#1 Material Master – QM view:

Inspection type ZP04(created above) is assigned to material) under quality view using MM01. inspection Type is assigned to the material master in SAP Quality Management so the system knows when and how quality inspection should be triggered for that specific material. It controls inspection lot creation, inspection stock posting, and quality processing during activities such as goods receipt, production, or warehouse movements. Without assigning an inspection type, SAP will not know that the material requires quality inspection.

#2 Maintain Inspection rule(/SCWM/QRSETUP):

Maintain Inspection Rule in SAP Extended Warehouse Management defines how the system should create and process inspections during warehouse activities.
It links warehouse number, inspection type, inspection object type (IOT), follow-up actions, and stock processing behaviour together.
The inspection rule controls when inspection documents are created and how stock is handled after Usage Decision (UD).

End to end Transactions:

#1 Inbound delivery creation(VL31N):

Inbound delivery is created with reference to purchase order and this should be distributed to EWM system. I am not using Handling unit(HU) in this example.

#2 Warehouse task creation(/SCWM/PRDI):

As a material receipt process, warehouse task needs to be created. And Inspection lot # 170000002613.

#3 Warehouse task confirmation (/SCWM/MON):

This step, warehouse task is confirmed.

 

 

#4 Warehouse stock(/SCWM/MON):

Below is EWM warehouse stock before quality stock

 

#5 Storage location stock(MMBE):

Below is storage location stock before quality check.

 

#6 Result recording(QA32):

User do record inspection characteristics such as measurements, defects, or test outcomes before completing the Usage Decision (UD). It helps SAP determine whether the material should be accepted, rejected, or sent for rework.

Select the Result recording

Entered the results

 

After Result recording, update UD code “A1” and then select “EWM Inspection”

 

Entered decision as “A01” and follow-up action as “EWM_A”

 

 

#7 Warehouse stock(/SCWM/MON):

After quality test, result recording and UD, EWM warehouse stock moved to F2(Un restricted)

 

#8 Storage location stock(MMBE):

Below is storage location stock after quality stock, Quality stocks has been moved to Unrestricted stock.

 

Conclusion:

The integration between SAP Extended Warehouse Management and SAP Quality Management in SAP S/4HANA provides a powerful framework for synchronizing warehouse execution and quality inspection processes within modern supply chain operations. Through proper configuration of inspection object types, inspection rules, inspection types, usage decision settings, and warehouse-dependent controls, organizations can achieve seamless quality inspection processing integrated directly with warehouse movements and stock management.

As demonstrated in this document, the end-to-end process starting from inbound delivery creation, warehouse task execution, inspection lot processing, result recording, and usage decision execution ensures accurate stock visibility, better traceability, and improved operational control across EWM-managed warehouses. A well-designed EWM-QM integration significantly improves inventory accuracy, inspection efficiency, compliance management, and overall warehouse productivity while reducing manual intervention and process inconsistencies.

 

Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, CIMCORP etc. He worked on various type of project flavours like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

entry.title: Re: SAP Convergent Charging HA setup: load balancer desig...
entry.content:
Hello

Find below few answers to your questions

  • In an SAP CC HA landscape with 3 dispatchers, 3 raters, 3 guiders, 3 bulkloaders and 2 updaters, how should the external load balancer be configured for updater Web Services?
    --> it should be configure in failover method. Meaning using first Updater by default and move to the second in case of failure.

  • Since the SAP CC updater has no real load balancing and only one updater should be active, should the load balancer target group contain only the currently active updater?
    --> see answer to question 1

  • Should the standby updater be completely excluded from the active load balancer target group until failover, or should it be included with a health check that understands the standby state?
    --> the second updater will be responsive as soon as the first one is off (not before). Checking is state when the first one is up is useless.

  • What is the recommended health check for updater Web Services? --> see the documentation "SOAP Connection Check"

  • If the static URL of the active updater is used and the updater still fails under load, what should be checked first?
    --> You have to the check the java GC behavior, the number of WS threads, the DB latency and the OS CPU. As all these points could be responsible of performance issue, you cannot prioritize and you have to check each items

  • Which Admin+ commands, indicators, traces or logs should be collected to prove whether the failure is caused by:
    --> see the SAP note 2667659 - Data to retrieve for a first level analysis of SAP Convergent Charging platform.

Best regards,

Eric F.

entry.title: Re: How to execute BD87 in Fogreground

entry.title: Re: Unable to fix plant wise profit center and Cost cente...
entry.content:
For  small price difference GL/ round off GL  in MIRO 

The automatic account assignment determination need to be defined in the highest level in Configuration Activity / SSCUI "Manage Default Account Assignments".

See kba 3322449 Account assignment errors when using detailed level

See also  3750765 - Balancing field "&1" is not filled in line item &2 raised for Supplier Invoice with minor differences

entry.title: Integrating Joule with SAP SuccessFactor
entry.content:
As organizations accelerate their digital HR transformation, the demand for intelligent, intuitive, and employee-centric solutions has never been higher. SAP Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot, brings a new layer of intelligence to SAP SuccessFactors by enabling natural language interactions, real-time insights, and guided decision-making across HR processes. Together, Joule and SuccessFactors redefine how HR teams and employees engage with talent data—shifting from transactional workflows to proactive, insight-driven experiences.

When integrated with SAP SuccessFactors, Joule acts as a smart HR assistant embedded directly into HR workflows. SAP Joule with SAP SuccessFactors transforms the way organizations manage and experience HR by combining intelligent automation with contextual, AI-driven insights. From enabling natural language queries and guided HR actions to delivering real-time workforce intelligence and seamless employee self-service, Joule simplifies complex HR processes across the organization. These capabilities not only empower HR teams and leaders to make faster, data-backed decisions but also reduce administrative workload, accelerate access to insights for managers, and enhance the overall employee experience through conversational HR. As a result, organizations achieve stronger adoption of SAP SuccessFactors features while unlocking greater strategic value from their HR technology investment.

Here, we outline the key steps involved in integrating SAP Joule with SAP SuccessFactors to enable intelligent, AI-driven HR experiences across your organization.

Pre-requisites:

  • SAP BTP Subaccount with Joule(das-application) Check the entitlements in your SAP BTP Global Account.
  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition / SAP Start.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services which usually will be delivered with SF tenant.

Roles required to configure:

  • SAP BTP Global Account Admin & BTP Subaccount Admin (in case they are different users)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Administrator
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services Administrator

Procedure:

  • Activating Joule and Running the Booster
  • Establish Trust with your Cloud Identity, BTP and Create Trusted Domains.
  • Activating Joule in SF.
  • Activate Cloud Foundry, Create Destinations and Work zone Content Provider.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Source & Target Configurations

License:

Joule is included as part of your SAP SuccessFactors license at no additional cost with a certain number of free messages for an annual period known as Base AI. Some of the AI Capabilities are part of the premium edition - Premium AI, where you may have to purchase AI Units to use the functionalities.

  • SuccessFactors customers will receive a Joule message allocation based on the number of licensed, active users; Users licensed for multiple products only count once.
  • Messages will be metered on an annual basis; at the end of each term, the annual allocation of free messages will reset (i.e., no carryover)
  • Customers must purchase additional SAP AI Units (Premium AI) if they exceed their free message allocation during the annual period
  • In case the service is not visible in your BTP Cockpit; to gain access to Joule, all customers will need to license a no-cost SAP SKU. Doing so will trigger the provisioning of Joule and the creation of the terms and conditions that must be accepted by the customer to use Joule.

Architecture Diagram:

 

Configuration Steps:

  • In BTP cockpit check if the Sucessfactor System is connected to BTP System Landscape and registered. If the system is not connected, we need to manually add the system to the landscape by registering the token successfully with SF.
  • Create a New subaccount in BTP cockpit for SF Joule.
  • Establish trust between the IAS and the BTP cockpit in the subaccount trusted configuration by adding the IAS URL.
  • Add the SF URL to the trusted domain in both BTP and IAS.
  • Create SAP Build Work zone service in the SF subaccount.
  • Run the Joule Booster.

 

Select the respective IAS, Build workzone and the SF tenant and finish the Booster run.

 

  • Login to SF with admin user and create a group, add the enable joule role to this group and assign to the users for enabling Joule icon. This group is required to be added to the users who are accessing the Joule to get the Joule Icon for them.

 

  • Enable cloud foundry and create a service key
  • A new role “Launchpad_Admin” will be created in BTP cockpit after creating the service key. Assign it to the configuration user before performing next steps.
  • The next step is to create destinations. Destinations LPS_SFSF_rt and SFSF_SSO are already created when running the booster.
  • Create a new destination named Navigation service. Open the service key and copy the portal service, client id, client secret and URL and paste in the destinations.
  • Create second destination named LPS_SFSFS_dt. For URL please follow the note 2215682.
  • In Build work zone URL and create a new content provider and give the design time and runtime destinations and check the report which will give the assigned roles imported to the content provider.

 

 

  • Login to IAS and check the sf.api.version for SF source system is 2. If it is 1 then we need to get the Json files from SAP portal for source and target systems and import it. For version 2 no action needed

 

Login to IAS and Import the downloaded Source and target file.

Then Download the certificate from IAS in source system and Import in SF.

 

 

  • Similarly Import the target file to IAS.
  • Finally run the read job in IAS to sync the configuration. We can see the logs from provisioning logs once it is completed. After the job is completed, we can see the roles assigned to specific users in work zone

Conclusion:

This completes the configuration of Joule for SF. Users can ask for SF queries to Joule and Joule will respond back. Please go through the blog and provide your valuable feedback and feel free to reach out to me in comment section for any doubts.

entry.title: Conversational Analytics on SAP S/4HANA CDS View Data with AI Claude Code and SAP BusinessObjects
entry.content:
Introduction

What if your business users could simply ask a question about their SAP S/4HANA data and get an interactive chart back without writing a single query, opening SAP Analysis for Office, or waiting for a new WebI report to be built?

That is exactly what I built: a prompt-driven analytics agent that sits on top of SAP BusinessObjects (BOBJ) and lets users explore S/4 CDS View data through natural language. In this post I will walk through the architecture, the two AI-powered flows at the heart of it, and what I learned along the way.

The Problem

SAP S/4 CDS View queries contain rich analytical data, but consuming that data typically requires either:

  • A pre-built WebI report — static, and requires BI developer involvement to change, or
  • Direct access to S/4 CDS views via Analysis for Office or other reporting tools — technical, not suitable for casual users.

Business users are stuck waiting. BI analysts are stuck building reports for every new question. This agent breaks that cycle.

What I Built

A browser-based agent - no SAP GUI, no thick client, that:

  • Connects to BOBJ via the REST API using standard username/password
  • Browses your existing WebI reports and lets you pick one as the analytical starting point, just as a base
  • Reads the S/4 CDS View query structure — dimensions, measures, variable prompts — directly from the report's data provider
  • Generates seed prompts using Claude: one clickable question per report block, written in plain business language
  • Answers free-form questions by translating natural language into drill/filter parameters and re-executing the query
  • Renders results as interactive Plotly charts (bar, line, pie, scatter) or HTML tables — all styled in SAP's brand palette

Screen Flow

The agent guides users through four screens from login to live interactive charts. Below are screenshots from a real session using a S/4HANA sflight data report.

Screen 1 — Login

The user enters the BOBJ host URL, authentication type, username and password. The agent calls the BOBJ REST logon endpoint and stores the session token server-side.

 

Figure 1 — Login screen: BOBJ host and credentials

Screen 2 — Report Picker

After login the user browses the BOBJ folder tree. Folders expand on click; .wid Web Intelligence documents are selectable. A preview panel shows the document name and ID before importing.

 

Figure 2 — Report picker: expanding the BOBJ folder tree

 

Figure 3 — A WebI report selected and ready to import

Screen 3a — Variable Prompts

Before the query is executed, the agent reads the report's mandatory and optional variable prompts and renders them as a form. The user fills in values (or leaves optional fields blank) and clicks Run Report.

 

Figure 4 —Variable prompt form: Departure and Arrival Airport filters

Screen 3b — Analytics

Once the report runs, the user lands on the main analytics screen. The left panel shows AI-generated seed prompts (one per report block) and a free-form chat input. The right panel shows the result, initially as a table, then as charts as the user explores.

 

Figure 5 — Initial table result after report execution, with seed prompt generated on the left

 

Figure 6 — Seed prompt panel: AI-generated prompt and free-form chat input

Interactive Charts in Action

Clicking a seed prompt or typing a free-form question triggers the query agent. Claude translates the natural language request into a structured query plan, the data is re-fetched from BOBJ, and Plotly renders the result instantly.

 

Figure 7 — Bar chart: Available Seats by Airline, generated from a seed prompt click

 

Figure 8 — Pie chart: Maximum Economy Capacity by Airline, from a follow-up question

Architecture

The stack is intentionally lean. All data access goes through BOBJ's REST API — the agent never connects to SAP S/4 directly. The queries are executed by BOBJ internally, which means existing security, authorizations, and variable prompts all work exactly as they do in WebI.

 

Browser (htmx + Plotly.js)
    │
    ▼
FastAPI Python App
    ├── bobj_client.py   →  SAP BOBJ REST API (Raylight)
    ├── import_agent.py  →  Claude API  (seed prompt generation)
    ├── query_agent.py   →  Claude API  (NL → query plan)
    ├── renderer.py      →  Plotly charts + HTML tables
    └── server.py        →  FastAPI routes + session state

 

The UI is a single HTML file using htmx for server-driven updates and vanilla JS for screen transitions, no frontend framework, no build step.

Two AI Flows

Flow 1 — Import: Turning a Report into Seed Prompts

When a user selects a WebI report, the agent calls four BOBJ REST endpoints to extract the block structure, Data provider dimensions and measures (with technical names and labels), and the Variable prompts.

 

This metadata is sent to Claude with a strict system prompt: generate one seed prompt per report block in plain business language, using only the dimensions and measures available in the schema.

 

For the flight data report, Claude produced:

  • Seed Prompt 1 — "Show Available Seats by Airline as Bar Chart"
  • Seed Prompt 2 — "Show the Maximum Capacity by Airline as Pie Chart"

 

These are not hardcoded — they are generated fresh from whatever report the user picks.

Flow 2 — Query: Natural Language to Parameters

Whether the user clicks a seed prompt or types their own question, the same flow runs:

  • Claude receives the user message, the CDS View schema (dimension and measure tech names + labels), active variable values, and the last 20 conversation turns.
  • Claude outputs a structured JSON query plan specifying data provider, filters, drilldown dimensions, measures, and chart type.
  • The server validates every dimension and measure tech name against the cached schema before touching BOBJ. If Claude hallucinated a field name, a one-time retry sends the error back for self-correction.
  • The validated plan is applied via BOBJ REST API and the result is rendered as a Plotly chart.

 

An example query plan output from Claude:

{
  "data_provider": "DP1",
  "filters": [
    {"dimension": "0DEPAP", "operator": "eq", "value": "FRA"}
  ],
  "drilldown": ["0AIRLINE"],
  "measures": ["AVAILABLE_SEATS"],
  "chart_type": "bar",
  "summary": "Available seats grouped by airline for flights departing Frankfurt."
}

 

The conversation is stateful, follow-up questions like 'Show as a pie chart instead' or 'Filter to Lufthansa only' carry full history and refine the previous query without starting over.

Key Engineering Decisions

  • Technical names over labels —  SAP technical names are the key to reliable LLM queries. Passing both tech_name and label to Claude — and requiring it to output only tech_name — dramatically reduces hallucination. The model maps 'airline' in the user's question to 0AIRLINE in the schema without guessing.
  • Variable scope injection —  Variable values must be part of every query context. If the user ran the report filtered to Frankfurt, every subsequent query includes that filter automatically. Injecting them as 'active variable filters' in the system prompt ensures consistent, scoped results.
  • The ## cell problem —  The BOBJ REST API returns ## for cells where the value is too large to display in the current drill state. The renderer detects and gracefully handles these rather than charting a string as a number.
  • Security by design —  No S/4 direct connection, no RFC. BOBJ's Raylight API is the only integration point, which means all existing S/4 authorizations and security apply automatically.

Project Structure

bobj-s4cds-agent/
  bobj_client.py      # BOBJ REST API client
  import_agent.py     # Seed prompt generation (Claude)
  query_agent.py      # NL → Query plan (Claude)
  renderer.py         # Plotly charts + HTML tables
  server.py           # FastAPI routes + session state
  static/
    index.html        # Single-page UI (htmx + Plotly.js)
  tests/
    test_bobj_client.py
    test_import_agent.py
    test_query_agent.py
    test_renderer.py
    test_server.py
  requirements.txt

 

To run locally:

pip install -r requirements.txt
export BOBJ_HOST=https://your-bobj-host
export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://your-hai-proxy/anthropic/
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=your-token
uvicorn server:app --reload

What's Next

This is v1 and deliberately scoped. Things could be added:

  • SAP SSO / SAML authentication — so users don't need a separate BOBJ password
  • Multi-data-provider joins — combine dimensions from different CDS Views in one chart
  • Saved queries / bookmarks — let users save useful prompts for quick reuse
  • Scheduled delivery — email a chart to stakeholders on a schedule

Closing Thoughts

The core idea here is simple: the CDS View query and its metadata are already a well-structured schema. An LLM is very good at mapping natural language to a structured schema. Connect the two with a thin validation layer and you get a surprisingly capable analytics interface — one that feels natural to business users and does not require a BI developer to answer every new question.

 

If you are working with SAP BOBJ and S/4 or BW, I hope this gives you a useful starting point. I hope you find this article informative and interesting.

entry.title: Re: How to execute BD87 in Fogreground
entry.content: Yes, the process you followed in BD87 is correct for debugging or identifying the exact point of failure in an errored IDoc. By selecting “Process in Foreground from Error,” the system stops at the error screen, allowing you to analyze where the issue is occurring. This is a standard approach used for online reprocessing and error analysis of IDocs.

entry.title: Re: User wants date range filed in maintenance order cost...
entry.content: What i was suprised about is that ITD (inception to date) was not in the sap default already included

entry.title: Re: Precision at Speed: AI‑Assisted Deal Sheet Generation in SAP Revenue Growth Management
entry.content: Every Key Account Manager in the consumer industry knows this challenge well. A deal sheet captures the agreed commercial terms — products, pricing, trade spend, sell-in dates, and conditions — before a promotion goes live. Yet, after negotiations are finalized, teams still spend countless hours manually copying pricing data across multiple systems and retailer-specific templates, often in different languages and formats.

entry.title: Re: Principal Propagation from SAP Build Process Automation workflow to S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
When building automated workflows in SAP Build Process Automation, it is often necessary to call SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud APIs on behalf of the actual business user rather than a shared technical account. This is where principal propagation becomes critical, as it securely forwards the user identity through the entire call chain, enabling S/4HANA to apply proper authorization checks, auditability, and segregation of duties.

Without principal propagation, all API calls appear under a generic service user, limiting security and traceability. In this article, I share the exact configuration steps required to enable principal propagation between SAP Build Process Automation and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, including SAP BTP trust setup, destination configuration, and S/4HANA communication arrangements.

entry.title: SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 : Deprecation & Removal of Key Features – What You Need to Know
entry.content:
As SAP BusinessObjects BI platform continues to evolve, it is essential for customers to stay informed about changes that impact existing workflows and system landscapes.

As part of this evolution, several legacy capabilities have now been marked as Deprecated in SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, while some features may eventually be Removed in future releases.

SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 introduces important deprecation announcements that customers should not ignore. While these features remain functional today, the long-term direction is clear: modernization, simplification, and adoption of open standards. Understanding these changes early helps organizations build a smoother roadmap for future upgrades and avoid last-minute migration challenges.

Understanding “Deprecated” vs “Removed”

Before reviewing the impacted features, let us first understand what these terms mean in the context of SAP product lifecycle management.

Removed

A feature classified as Removed has been completely eliminated from the product. Where possible, SAP documents recommended replacement solutions or alternative workflows.

Deprecated

A feature marked as Deprecated is still available and supported for current usage; however:

  • No significant future enhancements will be delivered
  • Innovation investment has stopped
  • The feature is expected to be removed in a future release

Customers are strongly encouraged to begin evaluating alternative solutions and transition plans as early as possible.

This approach is common across enterprise software modernization journeys — where legacy capabilities are gradually sunset to reduce technical debt, improve security posture, and align with modern architecture standards.

Deprecated Features in SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025

The following features have officially been marked as deprecated starting with SAP BusinessObjects BI platform 2025.

Deprecated Feature Details Recommended Alternative

 

Deprecated Feature
         Details                      Recommended Alternative
Visual DifferenceDeprecated in BI 2025 and planned for removal in future releasesNo direct alternative available
Workflow AssistantNo further innovation plannedUse standalone script packages leveraging the REST SDK to replace Workflow Assistant WebI Change Source templates. SAP may plan to publish samples on public GitHub.
PeopleSoft Integration KitLegacy integration approach being phased outUse standard authentication mechanisms such as OpenID. For Crystal Reports connectivity, connect directly to underlying databases using supported drivers such as Oracle DB, ODBC, or JDBC.
Web Application Split Deployment (Apache/Tomcat)Traditional split deployment architecture deprecatedUse regular web application deployment
DataDirect DriversDataDirect bundled drivers deprecatedUse certified native drivers such as:
  • MS ODBC Driver for SQL Server
  • Oracle ODBC/OLEDB drivers
  • DB2 native drivers
Or purchase DataDirect drivers directly from the vendor if required.
SAML AuthenticationSAML-based authentication deprecatedTransition to OpenID Connect / OAuth 2.0
WIDX in ZIP FormatZIP packaging format deprecatedUse WIDX in HTML format

Why Is SAP Deprecating These Features?

A common pattern across the deprecated capabilities is the move away from:

  • Legacy integrations
  • Older authentication frameworks
  • Complex deployment models
  • Vendor-specific middleware dependencies

towards:

  • Open standards
  • Cloud-ready authentication
  • Simplified deployment architectures
  • Native and certified connectivity options
  • REST API driven extensibility

This direction aligns with SAP’s broader technology strategy across analytics and enterprise platforms.

For example:

  • OpenID Connect and OAuth 2.0 provide more modern and secure authentication flows than traditional SAML implementations.
  • Native database drivers improve maintainability and certification alignment.
  • REST SDK-based automation offers greater flexibility compared to tightly coupled legacy assistants.

What Should Customers Do Next?

Organizations currently using these features should begin planning proactive transition activities.

Recommended Actions

1. Assess Current Usage

Identify whether any deprecated capabilities are actively used in your SAP BusinessObjects landscape.

2. Evaluate Replacement Solutions

Review SAP-recommended alternatives and validate compatibility with existing workflows.

3. Modernize Authentication

If using SAML or legacy integration kits, start evaluating OpenID Connect or OAuth 2.0 adoption strategies.

4. Test Before Upgrading

Validate migration approaches in non-production environments before future BI platform upgrades.

5. Monitor SAP Documentation

SAP may continue publishing additional migration guidance, GitHub samples, and KBAs related to these deprecations.

Strategic Perspective: From Legacy to Modernization

Deprecation announcements are not merely technical changes — they are signals for long-term platform direction.

Much like broader enterprise IT modernization initiatives, SAP is gradually transitioning customers from legacy operational models toward simplified, API-first, and standards-based architectures.

Rather than waiting for final feature removal, customers who start early will benefit from:

  • Reduced upgrade risks
  • Better security alignment
  • Simplified supportability
  • Improved future compatibility

A well-planned transition strategy can significantly reduce operational disruption when these features are eventually removed.

For further details, refer to:

  • BI 2025 What's New documentation
  • SAP KBA-3560025

Have you already started transitioning away from any of these deprecated features? Share your experiences and migration strategies in the comments below.

entry.title: Optimizer is failing in case of Inbound Consignment Order, having a carrier assigned.
entry.content:
We are receiving inbound ASN message via interface TransportationOrderGenericRequest_In creating a consignment order document in our system. This consignment order also receives carrier id. 

While planning the consignment order via optimizer (Selected Entries with All Resources), below message is displayed.

"None of the transportation demands can be planned".

When we remove the carrier from consignment order, optimizer is working fine.

We also tried using a freight unit which has carrier assigned, it is getting planned.

Note: We have tried both VSR_DEF and VSR_1STEP. 

Explanation Tool says No valid route that fulfills all constraints.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Find “Company Code Settings – Asset Account...
entry.content:
Dear @harsimran_singh,

If you cannot find app, first check the user’s business role/catalog authorizations assign in tenant

  • Maintain Business Roles app
  • Add role below
  • Inside role add catalog
  • Save and refresh tenant

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F7312')/S36

 

 

If the app is visible but the company code or ledger is missing inside the app then the issue is with the Asset Accounting company code/ledger setup, not with the configuration step itself

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2764392

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2909548

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: CHARM No project found
entry.content: SAP generally does not recommend performing system refreshes in landscapes managed by ChaRM, especially for the managed development system, as it can lead to data inconsistencies. If a refresh is unavoidable, it is recommended to follow SAP KBA 2259615 to resolve post-refresh inconsistencies and restore proper ChaRM functionality.

entry.title: Re: CHARM No project found
entry.content: This issue is commonly seen after a system refresh in ChaRM-managed landscapes, especially when project and logical component references become inconsistent. You may want to check SAP KBA 2259615 and verify project assignment, RFC connections, and ChaRM configuration consistency post-refresh.

entry.title: How to map ECC SPRO/IMG Activities to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SSCUIs - Looking for pract
entry.content:
Body:
Hi Community,
I'm working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation project where we are migrating from ECC 6.0. During the Fit-to-Standard (F2S) workshops, I need to create mapping documentation that shows:

ECC side: SPRO path / IMG Activity ID / Transaction Code
S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition side: SSCUI ID / Fiori App name / Configuration path

My Goal:
Create a comprehensive mapping table for FI module scope items (e.g., Accounts Receivable, Credit Management, General Ledger, etc.) to support:

Gap analysis documentation
BDCQ (Business-Driven Configuration Questionnaire) creation
Knowledge transfer from ECC consultants to Cloud consultants

What I've found so far:

SAP Notes mention "Configuration Activity.xlsm (SAP Customer)" in Roadmap Viewer
Some community posts reference the CUS_IMGACH table for reverse lookup
But I haven't found a ready-made, publicly available mapping document

My Questions:
1. Does such a mapping document exist?

Is there an official SAP document/spreadsheet that maps ECC IMG Activities to S/4HANA Cloud SSCUIs?
Or is "Configuration Activity.xlsm" the closest thing available?

2. If I need to use Configuration Activity.xlsm:

Step 1: Where exactly in Roadmap Viewer do I download it? (Which Roadmap? Which section?)
Step 2: Which sheet(s) should I look at? (I heard "IMG_Act BusinessCatalog Match" sheet exists)
Step 3: How do I search/filter to find SSCUI for a specific ECC IMG Activity or SPRO path?
Step 4: Can I export/pivot this data to create my own mapping table?

3. Alternative approaches:

Are there any SAP Help Portal pages that document SSCUI↔IMG relationships by module (FI/CO/SD/MM)?
Can I use Best Practice Explorer to find this mapping?
Should I check CBC (Central Business Configuration) documentation instead?
Any community-maintained spreadsheets or GitHub repos with this mapping?

4. Specific example:

ECC: SPRO → Financial Accounting → Accounts Receivable → Business Transactions → Outgoing Payments → Automatic Outgoing Payments → Payment Methods per Company Code
What is the equivalent SSCUI ID / Fiori App in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Background:
I'm an SAP FI consultant with strong ECC/on-premise experience but new to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. I can navigate SPRO blindfolded, but the Fiori-based configuration approach is still new to me.
Any concrete, actionable guidance with screenshots or step-by-step instructions would be greatly appreciated!
Thanks in advance for your help!
Best regards

entry.title: Re: How to apply SPO transport in prod system
entry.content: In SAP BW, transporting changes to a Semantically Partitioned Object (SPO) from Development to Production is the recommended and best practice approach rather than making direct changes in Production. However, if the SPO was previously modified directly in Production, there may be risks related to object inconsistencies, activation conflicts, or locking issues during transport import. It is advisable to compare object versions between systems and validate dependencies before transport execution. Would appreciate hearing others’ experiences with SPO transport handling in BW landscapes.

entry.title: Re: FB05 transfer posting with clearing does not update W...
entry.content: Thanks a lot for the clarification and detailed explanation. That confirms what we identified during our analysis as well: FB05 transfer posting with clearing is not payment-relevant from SAP standard perspective, therefore Extended Withholding Tax is not recalculated and WITH_ITEM is not updated. The distinction between: * clearing transactions and * payment-relevant transactions became much clearer after reviewing the SAP Notes and your explanation. In our case, the challenge is specifically related to Brazil IRF configured “at payment” within an ACM settlement process, where business expects withholding tax behavior during settlement/clearing operations. At this point, it seems the only standard-compliant approach would be redesigning the process to use payment transactions instead of transfer posting with clearing. Still, I would like to keep the discussion open to understand whether anyone has already implemented: * BTE/BAdI enhancement solutions; * custom WITH_ITEM generation; * custom payment-relevant logic; * or alternative approaches for Brazil localization scenarios. Especially in ACM or contract settlement integrations. Any shared experience or lessons learned would be greatly appreciated. Thanks again!

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP TRAIL Environment Not publishing any OData S...
entry.content:
I am experiencing the same issue today in SAP BTP ABAP Trial, system TRL, client 100, while working through SAP Learning “Basic ABAP Programming”.
An existing OData V4 UI service binding still works: ZUI_98FLIGHT_O4
To isolate the issue, I created a new OData V4 UI service binding on the same working RAP BO / service definition: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
ADT shows: Local Service Endpoint: Published
The generated inbound service also exists: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4_0001_G4BA
However, the Test fails with:
"Service group 'ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4' not published"
The ADT Feed Reader / Gateway Error Log shows:
  • Package: /IWFND/V4_COS_PUBLISHING
  • Application Component: BC-ESI-ESF-GW
  • Request Kind: Server OData V4
  • Service Name: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
  • Service Version: 0001
  • Group ID: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
Request URI: /sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_96flight_o4/srvd/sap/zui_96flight_o4/0001/$metadata?sap-documentation=all
Exception: /IWBEP/CX_V4_ACCESS_CHECK
  • Text: Service group 'ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4' not published
  • HTTP_STATUS_CODE: 404
  • Raise Location: /IWFND/CL_V4_PUBLISHING_DBA
The source extract shows that the runtime checks /IWFND/C_V4_MSGR:
SELECT SINGLE group_id FROM /iwfnd/c_v4_msgr
      INTO lv_group_id
      WHERE group_id = iv_group_id.
 
This check fails although ADT shows the service binding as Published.
Conclusion:
It looks like the ADT publish action updates the service binding object, but the OData V4 runtime publication entry is not created in /IWFND/C_V4_MSGR.
Old service bindings continue to work, but newly created service bindings do not.
Has anyone found a workaround for the ABAP Trial system?
One additional detail: a few days ago I ran “Check for Updates” in Eclipse and updated my ADT/Eclipse installation. I am not sure whether this is related, but the issue started after that timeframe.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Find “Company Code Settings – Asset Account...
entry.content:
Have you already accessed the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library?

If not, please follow these steps:

  1. Display the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library.
  2. Select the filter 'All apps for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud'.
  3. Choose the option: 'All apps'.
  4. Search for the app: 'Company Code Settings – Asset Accounting-Specific'.
  5. Read Make Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting-Specific at tab 'IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION'

I am experiencing the same issue today in SAP BTP ABAP Trial, system TRL, client 100, while working through SAP Learning “Basic ABAP Programming”.
I also commented on a similar existing thread, but I am opening a separate question because I have a reproducible case with a working old binding and a failing newly created binding on the same RAP BO.
An existing OData V4 UI service binding still works: ZUI_98FLIGHT_O4
To isolate the issue, I created a new OData V4 UI service binding on the same working RAP BO / service definition: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
ADT shows: Local Service Endpoint: Published
The generated inbound service also exists: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4_0001_G4BA
However, the Test fails with:
"Service group 'ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4' not published"
The ADT Feed Reader / Gateway Error Log shows:
  • Package: /IWFND/V4_COS_PUBLISHING
  • Application Component: BC-ESI-ESF-GW
  • Request Kind: Server OData V4
  • Service Name: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
  • Service Version: 0001
  • Group ID: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
Request URI: /sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_96flight_o4/srvd/sap/zui_96flight_o4/0001/$metadata?sap-documentation=all
Exception: /IWBEP/CX_V4_ACCESS_CHECK
  • Text: Service group 'ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4' not published
  • HTTP_STATUS_CODE: 404
  • Raise Location: /IWFND/CL_V4_PUBLISHING_DBA
The source extract shows that the runtime checks /IWFND/C_V4_MSGR:
SELECT SINGLE group_id FROM /iwfnd/c_v4_msgr
      INTO lv_group_id
      WHERE group_id = iv_group_id.
 
This check fails although ADT shows the service binding as Published.
Conclusion:
It looks like the ADT publish action updates the service binding object, but the OData V4 runtime publication entry is not created in /IWFND/C_V4_MSGR.
Old service bindings continue to work, but newly created service bindings do not.
Has anyone found a workaround for the ABAP Trial system?
One additional detail: a few days ago I ran “Check for Updates” in Eclipse and updated my ADT/Eclipse installation. I am not sure whether this is related, but the issue started after that timeframe.

entry.title: May Developer Challenge - Week 4: Storing vectors and enabling RAG
entry.content:
Welcome to the final week of the May Developer Challenge! 🎉

Links:

  • May's developer challenge blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/may-2026-developer-challenge-from-events-to-intelligence-building-an-event/ba-p/14386772
  • Week 1: Getting familiar with the events
  • Week 2: Connecting to the broker and consuming events
  • Week 3: Vectorizing the event payload
  • Week 4: Storing vectors and enabling RAG

This is where everything comes together. Events are flowing through a broker, being consumed from your code/by an integration platform, and being vectorized. Now we store those vectors in a vector store and wire up a simple RAG query to prove the pipeline is working end to end.

What is RAG?

Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) is a pattern where, instead of asking a large language model (LLM) to answer a question from memory alone, you first "retrieve" relevant context from a knowledge base and then "augment" the LLM's prompt with that context. The LLM generates a response "grounded" in your data rather than generating its response based on the data it's been trained with.

In our case, the "knowledge base" is the vector store we've been building — populated with embeddings of Business Partner events. A user could ask something like "Which business partners are in Germany?" and the RAG pipeline would:

  1. Embed the question using the same model you used in Week 3
  2. Search the vector store for the most similar Business Partner vectors
  3. Include those results in the LLM prompt
  4. Return a grounded, contextual answer

Your task this week

👉 Store the vectors generated last week into a vector store, then write a simple RAG query that retrieves relevant Business Partners based on a natural language question.

Steps:

  1. After generating the embedding in Week 3, store the vector along with the original event metadata (the data field) in a vector store
  2. Implement a query function: take a natural language question, embed it, search the vector store for the top-N most similar vectors, and return the associated Business Partner data
  3. (Optional but encouraged) Pass those results into an LLM to generate a natural language answer

Vector store options:

  • SAP options: SAP HANA Cloud - vector engine — SAP HANA Cloud has had native vector capabilities for some time now.
  • Open-source options: pgvector (Postgres extension)

Share your work

Reply to this post with:

  1. A screenshot or code snippet showing Business Partner vectors being stored in your vector store
  2. A sample query and the results returned (which Business Partners were retrieved?)
  3. (Optional) The LLM-generated answer if you wired up the full RAG response

Things to think about:

  1. What metadata did you store alongside the vector? Why does that matter for the quality of the RAG responses?
  2. How would you keep your vector store up to date as Business Partners change in the source system? (Hint: think about the event-driven pipeline you just built.)
  3. What's the difference between semantic similarity search and full-text search? In which scenarios would you prefer one over the other?

entry.title: Announcing General Availability of SAP Snowflake and SAP Business Data Cloud Connect for Snowflake
entry.content:
SAP is proud to announce two major milestones that move us closer to our vision of an open, partner-driven data ecosystem:

  • General availability of SAP Snowflake solution extension for SAP Business Data Cloud
  • General availability of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Connect for Snowflake

With this release, organizations can seamlessly combine mission critical SAP business data with Snowflake’s analytics and AI workloads, creating a unified, governed foundation for enterprise analytics, intelligent applications, and AI.

Introducing SAP Snowflake as an SAP Solution Extension

SAP Snowflake solution extension for SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) empowers customers with greater openness and choice while extending SAP BDC with Snowflake’s full capabilities.

With SAP Snowflake, customers gain access to the same enterprise-grade platform services and capabilities available in Snowflake’s Business Critical Edition. This includes Snowflake’s data warehousing, data engineering, AI, analytics, collaboration, Marketplace, and more—without any reduction in functionality. The difference lies not in what Snowflake can do, but in how it is sold, commercialized, and integrated within the SAP ecosystem.

SAP Snowflake is sold, contracted, billed, and supported by SAP, allowing customers to work with one vendor instead of two. In addition, data products can be shared directly between SAP BDC and SAP Snowflake without discrete data sharing fees, reducing cost, pipelines, and silos.

Furthermore, SAP Snowflake is fully integrated into the SAP experience—customers provision their SAP Snowflake tenant directly in SAP for Me, just like other SAP cloud services. There is no separate Snowflake account setup or manual onboarding required. Once provisioned, catalog integration with the Snowflake Horizon Catalog is done automatically.

As an SAP Solution Extension, SAP Snowflake is tightly integrated into the SAP BDC ecosystem, enabling:

  • Full native Snowflake capabilities, identical to standard Snowflake deployments
  • Native, zero copy access to SAP data products
  • Bidirectional data sharing between SAP and Snowflake
  • Preservation of business semantics, lineage, and governance
  • Seamless support for analytics, AI, and intelligent content

SAP Snowflake is ideal for customers who want the enterprise capabilities of Snowflake’s platform available as part of their SAP agreement and SAP managed environment.

SAP Snowflake Provisioning in SAP for Me

Zero‑Copy Data Sharing Between SAP Business Data Cloud and Snowflake

SAP BDC was designed to address the challenge of fragmented and de contextualized data by curating SAP data into business ready data products, enriched with business semantics, lineage, and governance. As a core feature of SAP BDC, BDC Connect and its partner integrations enable these data products to be shared securely and seamlessly with Snowflake AI Data Cloud platform without copies and data movement, enabling customers to activate trusted SAP data wherever analytics and AI innovation take place.

Through SAP BDC’s integration with Snowflake, organizations can:

  • Access SAP BDC data products directly within Snowflake
  • Share derived Snowflake data products back into SAP BDC
  • Simply their landscape by reducing costly data movement and duplication
  • Maintain consistent governance and business semantics across both ecosystems

This approach significantly reduces data engineering efforts and total cost of ownership while ensuring data remains consistent and up to date.

Snowflake Zero-Copy Connector for SAP BDC Connect

BDC Connect for Your Existing Snowflake Environment

If you already run Snowflake today, SAP BDC Connect for Snowflake allows you to seamlessly integrate your existing Snowflake environment with SAP BDC—without data replication and without changing your current Snowflake setup.

SAP BDC Connect for Snowflake follows the same proven onboarding approach introduced with SAP BDC Connect for Databricks, providing a consistent, secure, and repeatable enrollment experience across SAP’s partner integrations.

Getting started begins with a simple, one time enrollment process that establishes a secure, trusted connection between SAP BDC and your Snowflake account. Once enrollment is complete, this connection enables zero copy, governed data sharing between the two platforms.

Enrollment Flow

  1. The process starts in SAP for Me, where users provision a BDC Connect instance and specify the Snowflake account they want to connect. Users then move to Snowflake to create a Zero Copy connector object, which generates a unique connector identifier for the Snowflake account.
  2. That connector identifier is entered back into SAP for Me as part of the BDC Connect provisioning flow. In response, BDC Connect generates an invitation URL, which the user returns to Snowflake to complete the setup.
  3. When the invitation URL is applied in Snowflake, a secure handshake is performed using mutual TLS (mTLS) and the OpenID Connect (OIDC) protocol. This step verifies both systems and establishes mutual trust.
  4. Once the handshake completes successfully, the Snowflake Zero Copy connector is fully linked to the BDC Connect instance. At this point, the secure connection is in place, and customers can begin sharing SAP data products from SAP BDC and consuming them in Snowflake—confident that all data access is authenticated, encrypted, and governed from day one.

Sharing SAP Data Products to Snowflake

Data providers working in SAP BDC can easily share curated SAP data products with Snowflake through a simple, governed workflow.

The process typically involves:

  1. Selecting a data product within the BDC cockpit
  2. Choosing the Snowflake system where a trusted relationship has already been configured
  3. Initiating the sharing process

During the sharing process, SAP BDC publishes the metadata and business context associated with the data product, making it discoverable through Snowflake’s catalog integration. After the sharing process completes, the Snowflake administrator can see the data product as one of the available shares and mount it into the Snowflake Horizon Catalog as catalog linked database, where it becomes accessible to analytics and AI users.

To support different user personas and working styles, Snowflake offers multiple ways to discover and consume shared SAP data products.

  1. SQL Based Experience
    For technical users, available shares can be queried directly using standard SQL, enabling immediate access from familiar scripting and development environments. The SQL examples are available in the official documentation.
  1. AI Assisted Experience with Cortex AI
    With the Cortex AI Agent Cortex Code, users can provide instructions in natural language and have the required SQL generate automatically. This significantly lowers the barrier for non technical users and helps teams move from data access to insight more quickly.
  1. UI Driven Experience
    Users can also take advantage of a fully UI driven experience, allowing them to discover and consume shared data products directly through the interface—without writing any code.

 

Sharing Derived Data Products from Snowflake Back to BDC

The integration between SAP BDC and Snowflake is fully bidirectional, enabling a closed loop data lifecycle.

Customers can use Snowflake to:

  • Combine SAP and non SAP data
  • Create derived datasets
  • Generate AI based insights and features

These outputs can be built using data stored in Apache Iceberg 3.0, one of the leading open table formats, ensuring openness, interoperability, and long term flexibility across the data ecosystem. Once created, customers can package the results as derived data products, enriched with business semantics, rich metadata, and governance information.

The derived data products can then be published back into SAP BDC, where they become discoverable and reusable by downstream consumers across analytics, planning, and intelligent applications, extending the value of insights generated in Snowflake throughout the SAP landscape.

 

How to Choose Between SAP Snowflake and BDC Connect for Snowflake

The key difference between SAP Snowflake and SAP BDC Connect for Snowflake lies in how customers consume Snowflake—either as an SAP delivered solution or as an integration with an existing Snowflake environment.

With b, Snowflake is delivered as an SAP Solution Extension, meaning:

  • Snowflake is sold, contracted, billed, and supported by SAP
  • Customers work with one vendor instead of two
  • Snowflake is tightly integrated into the SAP BDC ecosystem

The table below highlights the key differences and benefits of each.

Capability Comparison

Capability

SAP Snowflake (SolEx)

SAP BDC Connect for Snowflake

Snowflake platform

Snowflake provisioned and delivered by SAP

Customer’s existing Snowflake environment

Commercial model

Single SAP contract and SAP billing

SAP contract for SAP BDC + existing Snowflake contract

Zero‑copy access to SAP data

Native access within SAP Snowflake

Enabled via BDC Connect

Bidirectional data sharing

Supported (Apache Iceberg 3.0)

Supported (Apache Iceberg 3.0)

Technical support

First-level support through SAP via Service

Supported (SAP for BDC Connect, Snowflake for platform)

SAP Snowflake (SolEx) is ideal for customers who want:

  • A fully integrated, SAP managed Snowflake experience
  • Consolidated procurement, billing, and support through SAP
  • No discrete data sharing fees (however infrastructure fees still apply)

SAP BDC Connect for Snowflake is ideal for customers who:

  • Already run Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud today
  • Want to continue existing Snowflake investments
  • Need secure, governed, zero copy access to SAP data products

Our partnership gives customers the freedom and flexibility to run data and AI workloads across SAP and non-SAP platforms—without compromising governance—by delivering a unified data  foundation for AI.

What’s Next: The Road Ahead

Last year, we announced the release of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Connect for Databricks, and now we’ve released SAP BDC Connect for Snowflake. We continue to move further towards our broader strategy to build an open, partner driven data ecosystem—one that enables customers to securely and seamlessly use their SAP business data across the platforms where analytics and AI innovation happen.

SAP will continue to expand the open data ecosystem by:

  • Extending SAP BDC Connect to additional partners such as Google BigQuery, Microsoft Fabric, and Amazon Athena.
  • Delivering a complete and intuitive UI experience to create and publish custom data products
  • Introducing data product request and approval workflows to allow users to create access requests using natural language

The focus remains on making governed data sharing easy, scalable, and accessible to all users.

In Summary

With the general availability of SAP Snowflake and SAP BDC Connect for Snowflake, SAP is delivering on its vision of an open, governed, AI ready data ecosystem.

Customers can now:

  • Share SAP data products into Snowflake using zero copy, bidirectional integration
  • Preserve business semantics, governance, and trust
  • Build analytics and AI on live, curated SAP data
  • Simplify procurement and operations with SAP Snowflake
  • Maintain flexibility with SAP BDC Connect for existing Snowflake environments

This is not just another integration—it is a foundational step toward turning trusted SAP data into a powerful engine for enterprise analytics and AI, wherever innovation happens.

For a deeper technical view, see the SAP Business Data Cloud reference architecture and SAP Snowflake documentation.

entry.title: Cash Flow Report Direct Method in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, 

Our customer has an internal requirment to create a cashflow-report with the direct method. They are currently running S4 public cloud with DRC activated for 2 out of 5 countries running in the system and they are located in EU: SE, DK, BE, NL & ES. Non of these countries has the cashflow report as a legal requirement. By reading about the cashflows I thought that we could use the Generic Cash Flow Statment (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4de200b26d5d42bbacae69c6bf6b9923/0f52ebd347db4943be45f7cff2bc2478.html) as a base. I follow the steps and then enter "Run Statutory Reports", when doing so I receive an error typing: "Assign Valid CF hier. ID value in Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting." When trouble-shooting I found KBA 3449130 which is exactly my error. When following the resolution it says: Change the ID in the configuration activity Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting (ID 101071) to the one that has already been maintained in the configuration activity Define Cash Flow Statement Versions for Several Countries/Regions(ID:105331)

I try to follow this but SSIUD 105331 isn't availble in our system even though J78 is active. When checking in the excel-list of configuration activities: 

It seems like the activity is "Specialized for Certain Countries" does this means that I'll not be able to use the generic cashflow since our countries isn't in the list?

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: The future of data is conversational and agentic AI — Are you ready?
entry.content:
Hi,

AI can significantly impact the role of data professionals by helping them make faster and better decisions through automation, data analysis, and intelligent insights.

This improves productivity and allows professionals to focus more on strategy, business context, and innovation instead of repetitive technical tasks.

The biggest gains are in areas such as anomaly detection, documentation, coding assistance, and exploratory analysis, but AI still falls short when dealing with business context, semantic understanding, and critical decision-making.

For this reason, I believe humans should remain in the loop, especially for critical tasks, validating results and intervening when necessary. A

I should support professionals, not fully replace human judgment and accountability.

entry.title: Re: Sourcing & Procurement in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hello @maugustin,

Thank you for raising this question! Based on the official SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release documentation (What's New Document PROC-4605-32B7), the "Transfer of Header Attachments from Purchase Requisitions to Purchase Orders" feature is designed to work with the interactive PO creation apps — specifically the Create Purchase Order – Advanced (ME21N) app and the Manage Purchase Orders app.

The Schedule Purchasing Jobs – Advanced creates purchase orders via a background/batch processing path, which operates independently of the interactive app layer where this attachment transfer mechanism is triggered. So the behavior you observed — attachments not transferring when POs are created via that batch job — is expected and by design, not a configuration gap.

There is also a configuration prerequisite for the feature to work on the interactive path: the same DMS document type must be maintained for both EBAN_HDR (PR header) and EKKO_PO (PO header) in your configuration environment. If you have not already done this, it may be worth checking — but this would not affect the batch job path.

Best regards,
Ashish Kakade
Cloud ERP & SCM Product Success

entry.title: Re: Custom header parameters in Gateway
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

I recently faced the same requirement to get the custom parameters from the ODATA request while using POST, when we test from gateway client. I solved it this using the below approach. Although, it's a short approach and also, we have a SLUG parameter available, but I didn't want to use it, as best practice would be to pass the actual custom name and value for the parameter in header. Hope this will help, refer below mentioned code and explanation.

pass a custom header by adding a new header to the request before executing, 
example: CUSTOM_FLAG, with value equals to X (Capital X), as it will be considered as abap_true.

in your redefined method for CREATE_STREAM, you must be having one parameter, io_tech_request_context
use this code to get it from headers.
*--------------------------------------------------------------
data : lt_headers type tihttpnvp,                   " internal table
           ls_header type ihttpnvp,                      " local work area
           lv_flag type flag.                                      " To store the flag value
lt_headers =  io_tech_request_context->get_request_headers( ).         " Will have all headers available here
read table lt_headers into ls_header with key name = 'CUSTOM_FLAG'.       " Pass the parameter name to read it
if sy-subrc = 0  and ls_header-value is not initial.                                                 " If available, assign it to local variable
    lv_flag = ls_header-value.
endif.
*--------------------------------------------------------------
thanks.
Kanwal

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer within the co code depreciation issue
entry.content:
Have you attempted the following steps in transaction ABUMN to address the depreciation discrepancies?

  1. Select the asset (formwork) from the asset master data.
  2. Define the distribution method (e.g., residual value-based or usage-based allocation).
  3. Execute the recalculation to redistribute depreciation costs proportionally.

This process sould ensure consistent depreciation allocation across projects while maintaining the overall depreciation rate.

  • Verify asset valuation settings in AW01N for accuracy.
  • Document adjustments in AW05 or AW07 for audit traceability

entry.title: Re: Pros and Cons of implementing full MDG within Central...
entry.content: Hi Imran, I agree with you that if MDG & CFIN are together there are certain tradeoffs for upgrade and user access etc. However, the critical piece is key mapping for master data objects. In a 3 way system whenever a new master data is created in MDG and replicated to source system, there also has to be a parallel trigger to CFIN and the mapping also has to be updated to ensure transactional data loads are not impacted due to mapping.

The error occurred because a part was missing in the previously implemented SAP Note "2673951 - TL API: Unable to load service lines during data migration of EAM general tasklist" for EA-APPL 605–617.

A new note "3754823 - TL API: Unable to load service lines during data migration" is released to complement the previous one, and it also needs to be implemented.

entry.title: Payment methods are not inherited from selected open items in the “Clear Incoming Payments” app
entry.content:
Dear All,

We are using the “Clear Incoming Payments” app to clear open items.

When we select original open items for offsetting/clearing, we expected some payment-related fields from the selected original line items to be inherited into the clearing/payment document. However, the following three fields are not inherited from the selected open items:

  • Payment Method
  • Baseline Date for Due Date Calculation
  • Cash Discount Days 1

Our main question is:

Is there any standard configuration, app setting, BAdI, substitution, or other enhancement option that allows these three fields to be inherited from the selected original open items during clearing in the “Clear Incoming Payments” app?

We would like to know whether SAP standard behavior intentionally does not copy these fields during clearing, or whether there is a way to control the field transfer.

The important point is that we are not simply asking why the fields are blank.
We specifically want to know whether there is any possible solution to make the following fields inherit from the selected source open item:

  1. Payment Method
  2. Baseline Date for Due Date Calculation
  3. Cash Discount Days 1

If this is standard behavior and these fields cannot be inherited in this app, we would also appreciate confirmation and any recommended workaround.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Are you wondering why you get the following message: Your SAP Universal ID is missing a second email
entry.content:
Are you wondering why you get the following message: Your SAP Universal ID is missing a second email address?  

 

Update Configuration option: Takes you directly into your Account Manager. Once logged in, you can click on, "Add identity" to add the additional email 

How to set up SAP Universal ID:  Will take you to a step by step video, explaining the linking process 

The most common issue people run into when they are adding a second account to their SAP Universal ID is that they add another email which has the domain as the primary email hence resulting in the error message that we see above. 

Here are some points to help prevent this: 

  • To stay certified, you must first pair at least two email addresses to the Universal ID.   but you must remember that Email domains must not match (Meaning a customer cannot use two @Gmail accounts to act as Primary and Recovery.) 
  • These two email addresses are not required to have any direct users attached to them. (S user or P user ID) 
  • These emails simply act as the Primary (First email) and Secondary (Also known as the Recovery email address) to assure the user has access. 
  • These emails must be in place first before a Certification can be added. 

Once you have followed the above steps your SAP Universal ID will be compliant and you will be able to stay certified. 

For more information, please refer also to the following documentation: 

  • 2882908 - How to add an additional user or e-mail address to the SAP Universal ID (UID) 
  • Step-by-step guide - How to assign a second email account to my Universal ID 

Find the Customer Interaction Center official page and contact information on the following link:  Contact Customer Interaction Center   

Thank you for reading! We hope you found this helpful and would love to hear your thoughts about this blog post. Let us know what you think!     

 Stay tuned for our upcoming Support Webinars and recorded sessions to continuously learn and get the best from SAP Support:  Register Now     

Read more #CustomerInteractionCenter blog posts here!

entry.title: Re: budget on Fixed Asset Purchasing in sap public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @MX2,

In Public Cloud budget availability control for direct Fixed Asset Purchasing with account assignment category A is not supported as standard. SAP confirms this specifically for the requested scenario fixed asset purchasing with Balance Sheet acquisition accounts and account assignment A. Purchase orders or purchase requisitions using a G/L account with cost element category 90 / Balance Sheet Account are not supported for this budget check. As a result a WBS element in combination with an asset is not supported for Budget Availability Control in this scenario.

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3597939

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3505874

This means that the individual objects may still exist in the system such as WBS, fixed asset, or PO with account assignment A. The limitation is the full end-to-end budget control process where the PO is posted directly to a fixed asset with account assignment A and a balance sheet acquisition account

Therefore the following on-premise-style process is not available end-to-end in Public Cloud standard:

  1. Statistical WBS used for budget availability control
  2. Budget uploaded for WBS + balance sheet asset acquisition account
  3. Final asset master containing the WBS
  4. PO created with account assignment A Asset
  5. Budget consumption/check during fixed asset acquisition

Recommended Public Cloud approach:

For fixed asset / investment budget control the supported design is to use Investment Projects in the Project Control Enterprise Projects app. The purchase should be posted with account assignment P Project / Investment Project, so the commitment and actual costs are controlled against the project budget first. The costs can then be settled to fixed assets / balance sheet accounts

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3215')/S36

Summary:

Direct budget availability control for fixed asset purchasing with account assignment A is not supported in Public Cloud. The supported design is Investment Project/WBS-based procurement with subsequent settlement to the fixed asset not direct AVC on account assignment A

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Open QP Settlements data migration – Responsibility, scope, and best practices
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am currently working on a data migration project and we have encountered a requirement regarding "Open QP Settlements" (Concentrate sales with an open Quotation Period / provisional pricing awaiting final settlement).

I would like to leverage the community's experience to clarify a few points:

  1. Migration Scope: Is the "Open QP Settlements" object typically included in the data migration load scope? Or is it standard practice to handle this differently (e.g., waiting for the settlement or handling it manually)?

  2. SAP Module / Responsibility: Which SAP module is usually responsible for this specific load? Is it owned by SD (Sales and Distribution), ACM (Agricultural Contract Management), CM (Commodity Management), or another specific module?

  3. Target Objects: Where exactly does this apply in the SAP target structure (which standard objects/tables or migration objects are used)?

If anyone has gone through this specific scenario in a previous Go-Live or migration project, I would highly appreciate it if you could share your experience, lessons learned, or any documentation references.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards, 
Valdir Brito Júnior.

entry.title: Profit Center Derivation Issue in New Intercompany Solution SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

After activating the new Intercompany (IC) solution in our sandbox system, we conducted testing and observed a change in system behavior related to Profit Center derivation in RV documents.

Observations:

  • In the new IC setup, the Profit Center for RV documents is derived from the Expense Invoice (ER).
  • In the old IC setup, the Profit Center was derived from the default Profit Center.

Impact:

Due to this change:

  • RV documents are posting to a different Profit Center.
  • KR documents are posting to the default Profit Center.

As a result, RV and KR documents are not aligned on the same Profit Center, whereas our requirement is that both document types should post to the same Profit Center.

As per SAP’s suggestion, we deactivated the node “Activate PC Derivation Based on Intercompany Sales Order Item (107061)”. However, this did not produce the desired results.

All combinations of test cases have been executed, and the results are attached for your reference.

We kindly request your guidance on how to ensure that both RV and KR documents derive and post to the same Profit Center.

 

Thanks

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Job-Specific Competencies Logic in PM Form
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
In SAP SuccessFactors Performance Management, our business requires that employees do not share a common set of competencies. Instead, competencies should be job‑specific, based on the employee’s job code. We plan to implement this using a template with job‑specific competencies.

However, we have identified a functional gap related to timing:
If an employee changes job code in December, and the performance form is launched in January, the system currently evaluates the employee using the new job‑specific competencies (December job code). While the employee to be evaluated based on the previous job code (the one valid before December).

We need a way to ensure that when the performance form is launched in January, the employee is evaluated using the job‑specific competencies associated with the job code they held before the change, not the new one.

We are looking for a solution or configuration option that enables the performance form to pull competencies based on the employee’s job code held for the longest period during the review year, rather than the job code active at the time the form is launched.

Any insights, experiences, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: 入出庫予定（保管場所間転送）を参照したWMS連携について
entry.content:
SAP MM / 外部WMS連携について質問です。

現在、保管場所間転送において、
「予定情報」をベースに外部WMS連携を行いたいと考えています。

実現したいこと：
① SAPから外部WMSへ入出庫予定情報を連携
② 外部WMSから実績としての入出庫情報を受信
③ 実績受信時にSAP側で入出庫伝票を登録

対象業務は保管場所間転送です。

現在、311移動などを確認していますが、
実績更新型のため、「予定情報」を保持した状態で外部WMSへ連携する方法が見当たりません。

そのため、以下を確認したいです。

・SAP標準で予定ベースの保管場所間転送を扱う方法はあるか
・ある場合、必要なConfig／マスタ設定は何か
・難しい場合、一般的な代替案は何か
　（STO、配送伝票、WM/EWM利用、アドオン等）

実案件での構成例やベストプラクティスがあればご教示いただきたいです。

よろしくお願いいたします。
